page: Vultr vbm-128c-2048gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7713 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system features 128 physical cores and 256 vCPUs with 2.0 threads per core, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated, ensuring no resource contention from other tenants.

This server is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is provided by a local 960 GB SSD. The configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no graphics processing units (GPUs) bundled with this server configuration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers high resource density, combining a massive memory footprint with a high physical core count on dedicated hardware. This eliminates virtualization overhead and noisy-neighbor issues. The hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, virtualization hosting, enterprise applications, and heavy multi-threaded compute tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-128c-2048gb-amd is a dedicated bare metal server from the Bare Metal SSD family, designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7713 processor with 128 physical cores and 256 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server features 2048.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a local 960 GB SSD for storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration. With its dedicated CPU allocation and massive memory capacity, this server provides high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead. It is suited for large-scale databases, memory-optimized enterprise applications, virtualization hosting, and highly parallelized compute workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring 128 AMD EPYC cores, two terabytes of system memory, and local solid-state storage.
